MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-16-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Nevin of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Monica Williams of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith (362.2580) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted as to claims for false representation and premises liability and denied as to other issues. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence on the summary judgment motion. Plaintiff's declaration is admissible as is the Ay declaration and both create a triable issue of fact. On punitive damages, defendant's special interrogatories do not address that issue. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-10-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHNSON CONTROLS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: Oren P. Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of producing evidence. The questioning of plaintiff is less probative than that deemed not to shift the burden in Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1433, 1439. In addition, defendant did not submit the interrogatories that were propounded. Absent the questions, the Court is unable to conclude that the responses are factually devoid. Further, plaintiff's declaration is admissible based on having his recollection refreshed. That testimony creates a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff to submit proposed form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
